schema.appDescription: Calculate monthly TDS deducted from salary under Section 192. See annual tax, monthly TDS, and net in-hand salary for FY 2026-27.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the TDS on Salary Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: How is TDS on salary calculated in India?
faq.a: Your employer calculates expected annual income, applies applicable deductions (based on your investment declaration), computes annual tax under the selected regime, and divides by 12 to arrive at monthly TDS. The employer adjusts TDS in later months if actual investments differ.

faq.q: What is Form 16 and why is it important?
faq.a: Form 16 is the TDS certificate issued by employers annually by 15 June. It contains salary details (Part A from TRACES) and salary computation with deductions (Part B). It's the key document for filing your ITR and serves as proof of TDS payment.

faq.q: Can I reduce TDS on salary?
faq.a: Yes. Submit Form 12BB to your employer with investment declarations — PPF, ELSS, LIC premiums (80C), health insurance (80D), HRA details, home loan interest. The employer reduces TDS accordingly. Declare accurately — under-declaration leads to underpaid TDS.

faq.q: Is the TDS on Salary Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, the TDS on Salary Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the TDS on Salary Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh/month salary — TDS calculation
examples.text: For ₹1 lakh/month gross salary (₹12 lakh annual), new regime tax after ₹75,000 standard deduction is on ₹11.25 lakh = ₹86,250. Monthly TDS = ₹7,188. Net in-hand ≈ ₹92,812/month.

examples.heading: ₹2 lakh/month salary — 30% bracket
examples.text: ₹2 lakh/month (₹24 lakh annual) under new regime: tax on ₹23.25 lakh ≈ ₹4,32,500. Monthly TDS = ₹36,042. Net in-hand ≈ ₹1,63,958 before other deductions like PF, professional tax.
